questions.question: What is the Virtual DOM?
options: B) In-memory JS representation of real DOM — React diffs it to minimize actual DOM updates
questions.correctAnswer: B) In-memory JS representation of real DOM — React diffs it to minimize actual DOM updates

questions.question: What is reconciliation?
options: B) React's diffing algorithm comparing old and new virtual DOM to determine minimal real DOM updates
questions.correctAnswer: B) React's diffing algorithm comparing old and new virtual DOM to determine minimal real DOM updates

questions.question: Why are keys important in lists?
options: B) Help React identify which items changed/added/removed — stable keys prevent unnecessary re-renders
questions.correctAnswer: B) Help React identify which items changed/added/removed — stable keys prevent unnecessary re-renders

questions.question: What is JSX?
options: B) JavaScript syntax extension that looks like HTML — Babel compiles it to React.createElement() calls
questions.correctAnswer: B) JavaScript syntax extension that looks like HTML — Babel compiles it to React.createElement() calls

questions.question: What is prop drilling?
options: B) Passing props through multiple intermediate components that don't use them — just to reach deep child
questions.correctAnswer: B) Passing props through multiple intermediate components that don't use them — just to reach deep child

questions.question: What is a controlled component?
options: B) Form element whose value is controlled by React state — input value always reflects state
questions.correctAnswer: B) Form element whose value is controlled by React state — input value always reflects state

questions.question: What triggers a React re-render?
options: B) State change, prop change, parent re-render, or context change
questions.correctAnswer: B) State change, prop change, parent re-render, or context change

questions.question: What does React.memo() do?
options: B) HOC that memoizes component — skips re-render if props haven't changed (shallow comparison)
questions.correctAnswer: B) HOC that memoizes component — skips re-render if props haven't changed (shallow comparison)

questions.question: What is a React Fragment?
options: B) Wrapper (<> </> or <Fragment>) grouping multiple children without adding extra DOM node
questions.correctAnswer: B) Wrapper (<> </> or <Fragment>) grouping multiple children without adding extra DOM node

questions.question: What is an error boundary?
options: B) Class component with componentDidCatch that catches JS errors in child tree and shows fallback UI
questions.correctAnswer: B) Class component with componentDidCatch that catches JS errors in child tree and shows fallback UI

questions.question: What is React.lazy() used for?
options: B) Dynamic import of component — enables code splitting so component loads only when needed
questions.correctAnswer: B) Dynamic import of component — enables code splitting so component loads only when needed

questions.question: What is the children prop?
options: A) Array of child components only
B) Special prop containing everything between component's opening and closing JSX tags
questions.correctAnswer: B) Special prop containing everything between component's opening and closing JSX tags

questions.question: What is a Higher Order Component (HOC)?
options: B) Function that takes component and returns new enhanced component — pattern for reusing logic
questions.correctAnswer: B) Function that takes component and returns new enhanced component — pattern for reusing logic

questions.question: When does React batch state updates?
options: B) In React 18+, all updates are batched by default; earlier only in React event handlers
questions.correctAnswer: B) In React 18+, all updates are batched by default; earlier only in React event handlers

questions.question: What is the render prop pattern?
options: B) Sharing code between components using prop whose value is a function returning JSX
questions.correctAnswer: B) Sharing code between components using prop whose value is a function returning JSX

questions.question: What is a React portal?
options: B) Renders children into different DOM node outside parent hierarchy — used for modals/tooltips
questions.correctAnswer: B) Renders children into different DOM node outside parent hierarchy — used for modals/tooltips

questions.question: What is the difference between defaultProps and TypeScript default params?
options: B) defaultProps is React-specific class/function prop defaults; TS default params are JS-level defaults in function signature
D) defaultProps is deprecated in function components
questions.correctAnswer: D) defaultProps is deprecated in function components

questions.question: What is Concurrent React?
options: B) React 18 feature enabling interruptible rendering — React can pause and resume work based on priority
questions.correctAnswer: B) React 18 feature enabling interruptible rendering — React can pause and resume work based on priority

questions.question: What is the Suspense component?
options: B) Shows fallback UI while children are loading (lazy components or data fetching)
questions.correctAnswer: B) Shows fallback UI while children are loading (lazy components or data fetching)

questions.question: What does StrictMode do in React?
options: B) Development-only wrapper that double-invokes functions/effects to detect side effects and deprecated APIs
questions.correctAnswer: B) Development-only wrapper that double-invokes functions/effects to detect side effects and deprecated APIs

questions.question: What is the difference between useState and useRef?
options: B) useState triggers re-render on update; useRef persists mutable value across renders without re-render
D) useState is for DOM only
questions.correctAnswer: B) useState triggers re-render on update; useRef persists mutable value across renders without re-render

questions.question: What does empty dependency array [] in useEffect mean?
options: B) Effect runs only once after initial mount — like componentDidMount
questions.correctAnswer: B) Effect runs only once after initial mount — like componentDidMount

questions.question: What is the useEffect cleanup function?
options: B) Function returned from useEffect — runs on unmount and before next effect execution
questions.correctAnswer: B) Function returned from useEffect — runs on unmount and before next effect execution

questions.question: What is useMemo vs useCallback?
options: B) useMemo memoizes computed VALUE; useCallback memoizes FUNCTION reference
questions.correctAnswer: B) useMemo memoizes computed VALUE; useCallback memoizes FUNCTION reference

questions.question: When should you use useReducer over useState?
options: B) When state logic is complex with multiple sub-values or next state depends on previous state
questions.correctAnswer: B) When state logic is complex with multiple sub-values or next state depends on previous state

questions.question: What is the stale closure problem?
options: B) Effect/callback captures outdated value from previous render — fixed by adding to dependency array
questions.correctAnswer: B) Effect/callback captures outdated value from previous render — fixed by adding to dependency array

questions.question: What is useLayoutEffect vs useEffect?
options: B) useLayoutEffect fires synchronously after DOM mutations before browser paint — blocks visual update
questions.correctAnswer: B) useLayoutEffect fires synchronously after DOM mutations before browser paint — blocks visual update

questions.question: What is a custom hook?
options: B) Reusable function starting with 'use' that can call other hooks — extracts stateful logic
questions.correctAnswer: B) Reusable function starting with 'use' that can call other hooks — extracts stateful logic

questions.question: What is useContext used for?
options: B) Consuming React Context — avoids prop drilling by accessing context value anywhere in component tree
questions.correctAnswer: B) Consuming React Context — avoids prop drilling by accessing context value anywhere in component tree

questions.question: What does useState's functional update form do: setState(prev => prev + 1)?
options: B) Ensures update is based on latest state — prevents stale state in async/batch updates
questions.correctAnswer: B) Ensures update is based on latest state — prevents stale state in async/batch updates

questions.question: What is useImperativeHandle?
options: B) Customizes instance value exposed by useRef when used with forwardRef — controls imperative API
questions.correctAnswer: B) Customizes instance value exposed by useRef when used with forwardRef — controls imperative API

questions.question: What is forwardRef?
options: B) Allows component to forward its ref to child DOM element or component
D) Passing refs as props directly
questions.correctAnswer: B) Allows component to forward its ref to child DOM element or component

questions.question: What does the Context API solve?
options: B) Provides way to pass data through component tree without prop drilling — global state for React
questions.correctAnswer: B) Provides way to pass data through component tree without prop drilling — global state for React

questions.question: What is Zustand?
options: B) Lightweight state management library — simpler than Redux, uses hooks, minimal boilerplate
questions.correctAnswer: B) Lightweight state management library — simpler than Redux, uses hooks, minimal boilerplate

questions.question: What is Redux Toolkit (RTK)?
options: B) Official opinionated Redux toolset reducing boilerplate — includes createSlice, createAsyncThunk
questions.correctAnswer: B) Official opinionated Redux toolset reducing boilerplate — includes createSlice, createAsyncThunk

questions.question: What is the difference between local and global state?
options: B) Local state lives in one component (useState); global state shared across components (Context/Redux/Zustand)
C) Global state is in database
questions.correctAnswer: B) Local state lives in one component (useState); global state shared across components (Context/Redux/Zustand)

questions.question: What does useEffect with no dependency array do?
options: B) Runs after EVERY render — equivalent to componentDidUpdate for all updates
D) Runs only on state change
questions.correctAnswer: B) Runs after EVERY render — equivalent to componentDidUpdate for all updates

questions.question: What is the exhaustive-deps ESLint rule for?
options: B) Warns when useEffect/useCallback/useMemo dependency arrays don't include all used variables
questions.correctAnswer: B) Warns when useEffect/useCallback/useMemo dependency arrays don't include all used variables

questions.question: What is code splitting in React?
options: B) Breaking app into smaller chunks loaded on demand — reduces initial bundle size using React.lazy/Suspense
questions.correctAnswer: B) Breaking app into smaller chunks loaded on demand — reduces initial bundle size using React.lazy/Suspense

questions.question: What is the difference between useEffect and event handlers for data fetching?
options: B) useEffect fetches on render/mount; event handlers fetch on user action — choose based on trigger
C) Event handlers are always better
D) useEffect is deprecated for fetching
questions.correctAnswer: B) useEffect fetches on render/mount; event handlers fetch on user action — choose based on trigger

questions.question: Which hook is used for state in React?
questions.correctAnswer: B) useState

questions.question: Which React hook runs after render?
questions.correctAnswer: B) useEffect
